rulings.Case Title: ASIAN AMERICANS ADVANCING JUSTICE - LOS ANGELES VS. ALEX PADILLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2018, Line 8, RESPONDENT ALEX PADILLA'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Defendant secretary of state demurs on one ground: "that the allegations fail to state sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action" - CCP §430.10(e). The demurrer is OVERRULED. At a minimum, a petition for writ of mandate is adequately pled. Procedures applicable to demurrers in ordinary civil actions also apply to administrative mandamus proceedings. (See CCP §1109.) Thus, I may consider only the pleading's contents, and all of its allegations are deemed true. (Kleiner v. Garrison (1947) 82 Cal.App.2d 442, 445.) The secretary requests that I adopt his view of contents of certain documents, but judicial notice applies only to facts not reasonably subject to dispute. (See, e.g., Jefferson, Cal. Evidence Benchbook (4th ed. 2009) Judicial Notice, §49.10, p. 1147.) The secretary is to file an answer on or before July 6, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SILVA VS. THE CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS EDWARD SILVA, EVELYN SILVA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Plaintiffs Edward and Evelyn Silva seek summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication on their complaint for a tax refund. Plaintiffs assert that defendant California Franchise Tax Board did not mail them a notice of proposed assessment (NPA) for a purported 2009 tax deficiency and, even if it did, the NPA was mailed to the wrong address, voiding the assessment. In February 2014, an FTB tax auditor notified the Silvas' tax representative, CPA John Lavorato, that the FTB's audit of the Silvas' 2009 taxes was being closed and a NPA would issue within 90 days, after which the Silvas would have 60 days to file a protest. (Li Dec. 7 & Ex. E.) The Silvas assert that the FTB "never issued" a NPA and, if it did, the NPA "was never mailed." (RUMF 7.) The FTB disputes these allegations, citing documentation that includes a certificate of mailing the NPA. (RUMF 7, 9, 22) These are triable issues of material fact that preclude summary adjudication. (See CCP §437c.) The summary judgment record is also rife with other triable factual disputes. For example: (1) did the Silvas intend to "mandate the FTB to send all notices and communications" to Lavorato? (2) what did the FTB's communications with Lavorato include? (3) was the FTB purporting to "compl[y] with Form 3520"? (4) did the Silvas receive a copy of the NPA before October 24, 2014? (5) when was the FTB's last communication with Lavorato? (6) was a regular procedure followed for collecting the Silvas' mail, and, if so, what was it? (RUMF 4, 5, 6, 9, 10, 11, 12.) The issues of law raised by the summary judgment papers may also be addressed at trial. Plaintiffs' motion is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SEYEDEH KALANTARI VS. BLOOMINGDALE'S INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions, For Interrogatories, And Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF SEYEDEH KALANTARI'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions, For Interrogatories, And Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is GRANTED: Defendants' objections to RFA's Nos. 7, 8, and 9, are overruled as lacking merit, and further written responses to Form Interrogatories No. 17.1 is required, with regard to defendants' denials of RFA's Nos. 7, 8, and 9. Defendant's witness, Mr. Spaulding, testified that plaintiff's absences "forc[ed] us to engage in less-safe apprehensions...," putting that contention at issue. Plaintiff is entitled to obtain substantive responses to discovery regarding that contention. The responses provided by defendant to Form Interrogatory No. 17.1 in this regard are evasive and do not provide the required information. Likewise, further written responses are required to Interrogatories Nos. 27, 28, and 29, given defendants' denials of RFA's Nos. 7, 8, and 9. Defendants' privacy objection is overruled, as the cited Penal Code Sections do not apply to a private company who apprehends a shoplifter; in addition, a Protective Order s already in place that can support limited use of the required information, for the purposes of this litigation only, and in accordance with the terms of that Protective Order. Defendants' other objections are also overruled. Further written, substantive, and verified responses to the Form Interrogatory No. 17.1 with regard to RFA's Nos. 7, 8, and 9, and to Special Interrogatories Nos. 27, 28, and 29, are required within 20 days of the date of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY SHADDOCK VS. SCOTT KAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action And Compel Arbitration Of Shaddocks Claims Against Laguna
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANTS SCOTT KAY, DEBORAH KAY, 1833 LAGUNA LLC'S d Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action And Compel Arbitration Of Shaddocks Claims Against Laguna. In a dispute over a construction renovation project, defendant 1833 Laguna LLC moves to arbitrate five of the causes of action at issue and to dismiss or stay this court action. In their construction contract's arbitration agreement, the parties stipulated that "this agreement to arbitrate shall be governed by the Federal Arbitration Act." A trial court's "only option in these circumstances is to stay the court proceeding and compel the arbitration." (Rodriguez v. American Technologies, Inc. (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 1110, 1115.) As here, the party opposing arbitration in Rodriguez tried to rely on Code of Civil Procedure §1281.2(c), but the Court of Appeal ruled that the FAA's procedural provisions control over state law. (138 Cal.App.4th at 1115, 1122.) Plaintiff points to a statement in another part of the construction contract: "this Agreement shall be governed by the law in effect at the location of the Project" - i.e., California law. However, this provision is outside the parties' agreement to arbitrate and does not mention arbitration; it clearly refers to substantive, not procedural, law. (See Civil Code §1641 ("whole of contract is to be taken together, so as to give effect to every part").) "Thus, there is no ambiguity regarding the parties' intent. They adopted the FAA - all of it - to govern their arbitration." (Rodriguez, 138 Cal.App.4th at 1122.) The motion to stay this court action and to compel arbitration is GRANTED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID MOURRA ET AL VS. ROMAN SHTYLMAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANTS ROMAN SHTYLMAN, BITFLOOR, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Lack of personal jurisdiction over a defendant is not a ground for demurrer. It must be raised, if at all, by a motion to quash service of summons. (CCP §418.10; Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) §7:65.) Defendants' reply brief asserts that jurisdiction is lacking over the Poe plaintiffs, but this is not a ground stated in defendants' demurrers. Plaintiffs adequately plead alter ego and veil piercing theories. Defendants say these claims will not be proven, but such argument is not for the pleading stage. Under these theories, a court may disregard the corporate entity and treat the acts as if they were done by the individuals. (See Shapoff v. Scull (1990) 222 Cal.App.3d 1457, 1466, 1469.) As to the individual causes of action, defendants misapprehend California pleading law. "To survive a demurrer, the complaint need only alleged facts sufficient to state a cause of action; each evidentiary fact that might eventually form part of the plaintiff's proof need not be alleged." (C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 872.) Moreover, where, as here, all the demurrers are general demurrers under Code of Civil Procedure §430.10(e): "Objections that a complaint is ambiguous or uncertain, or that essential facts appear only inferentially, or as conclusions of law, or by way of recitals" may not be raised. (Johnson v. Mead (1987) 191 Cal.App.3d 156,160.) 1st & 5th (Conversion; Negligence): The claims are adequately pled under California law. Gomes v. Countrywide Hone Loans, Inc. (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 1149 does not, as defendants assert, hold that "the source" of an allegation must be pled. 2nd (Breach of Contract): The claim is adequately pled, and Shtylman may be liable under the complaint's alter ego theory. 3rd & 4th (Open Book Account; Account Stated): The claims are adequately pled. It does not matter that the "book" and/or "account" may be electronic. 6th & 7th (Fraud; Fraudulent Transfer): The claims are pled with sufficient specificity. 8th (Conspiracy): The claim is adequately pled. More than a conspiracy between Shtylman and Bitfloor is alleged, and defendants may be liable for conspiracy with third parties. 9th (Breach of Fiduciary Duty): The claim is adequately pled, and Shytlman may be liable under the complaint's alter ego theory. Defendants' demurrers are OVERRULED in their entirety. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ALI A. AALAEI ET AL VS. JOSEPH GAROFOLO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Complaint And Related Filing; Memorandum Of Points And Autorities; Declaration Of Babak Hashemi, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2018, Line 9, PLAINTIFFS ALI AALAEI, ARI LAW, P.C. Motion To Seal Complaint And Related Filing. Plaintiffs' motion "to seal complaint and related filings" is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Plaintiffs filed their complaint in unredacted form back on April 30. For the eight-plus weeks since then, the complaint has been publically available on the Court's register of actions - apparently waiving any confidentiality that could ever have existed. Further, my review of the public complaint finds no immediately apparent "overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record." (See CRC 2550.) I also agree with defendant's concern that granting the pending motion would give plaintiffs "a blank check to unilaterally file under seal any future filing of their and their counsel's choosing if they, in their sole discretion, believe it should be shielded from public view." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
